preview_text:
The model favors Uruguay at 74%, leaving Cape Verde with a 5% chance of victory. The underdog's low win probability reflects a substantial gap in the forecast, though a draw registers at 21% and remains a meaningful outcome.

The win–draw–loss spread shows Uruguay as clear favorite, yet the draw probability suggests the match has room for a competitive contest. Cape Verde's 5% win chance indicates the model sees an asymmetric contest, but the 21% draw rate signals neither team is overwhelmingly favored to settle matters inside normal time.

The model and sportsbook diverge meaningfully on Cape Verde's chances. The market consensus places the underdog at 12%, some 7 percentage points above the model's 5%. The market also assigns slightly lower odds to a Uruguay win (66% vs 74%). Polymarket pricing aligns more closely with the model on the draw and underdog win probability. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct.
